title: calamagrostis langsdorffii var. marylandica
intro: Calamagrostis langsdorffii var. marylandica, also known as Maryland fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, this plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal value. The health benefits associated with Calamagrostis langsdorffii var. marylandica include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
